steps.title: Check the domain
steps.content: Both functions have base $1.3$, which is greater than $1$. Therefore both functions increase as $x$ increases. The domain is $x\gt0$, so $x=0$ is not included.

steps.title: Analyze the first function
steps.content:
For $f(x)=20(1.3)^{x+4}$, the smallest approached value would occur as $x$ approaches $0$ from the right:
$$20(1.3)^4$$
That value is not reached because $x\gt0$.

steps.title: Analyze the second expression
steps.content:
The same domain issue applies to $g(x)=20(1.12)(1.3)^{x+2}$. Its lower boundary is approached near $x=0$:
$$20(1.12)(1.3)^2$$
The function still has no actual minimum on the domain.

steps.title: Decide what is displayed
steps.content: Neither equation displays an actual minimum value as a constant or coefficient on the given domain. The answer is D.
